admin_panel.notes: Netgear's default credentials are admin/password. Newer firmware versions require changing the admin password during first-time setup. If the password was changed and forgotten, a factory reset restores admin/password. The Nighthawk app can also be used for initial setup but the full web admin panel is always available at routerlogin.net.

ethernet.nav_path: Basic > Internet (or ADVANCED > Setup > Internet Setup)
ethernet.connection_type_field: Does your Internet connection require a login?
ethernet.ipoe_label: No (DHCP is the default when login is not required)
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from the nbn connection box to the yellow WAN/Internet port on the rear of the router
Plug in the power cable and turn on the router
Wait 1-2 minutes for the router to boot — the Power LED should turn solid
Connect a device to the router via Ethernet or Wi-Fi (default Wi-Fi credentials are on the router's label)
Open a web browser and go to http://routerlogin.net (or http://192.168.1.1)
Log in with username 'admin' and password 'password' (or the password you set during initial setup)
Click 'Basic' tab, then 'Internet' in the left menu
For 'Does your Internet connection require a login?', select 'No'
Ensure no PPPoE username/password fields are filled in
Wait 1-2 minutes for the router to obtain an IP address and connect

wan_config.vlan_notes: No VLAN configuration needed for Belong. Netgear Nighthawk routers do not prominently expose VLAN settings in the standard UI.

physical.wan_port_position: Yellow Ethernet port on rear panel, typically rightmost or separated from the LAN ports
physical.wan_port_notes: The Internet/WAN port is yellow and clearly labeled 'Internet'. Do not confuse with the LAN ports which are also Ethernet but numbered 1-4.

factory_reset.method: Press and hold the Reset button on the rear panel for at least 10 seconds until the Power LED starts blinking. Release and wait for the router to reboot to factory defaults.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset restores admin/password credentials and clears all settings including Wi-Fi name/password and ISP configuration. The router will re-enter initial setup mode.

firmware.check_path: ADVANCED > Administration > Firmware Update (or Router Update)
firmware.notes: Netgear supports online firmware check from the admin panel. The Nighthawk app also provides firmware update notifications. Update to latest firmware before configuring for Belong.

setup.setup_notes: The Netgear RAX50 is a straightforward standalone router. Netgear's WAN configuration uses an unusual field label: 'Does your Internet connection require a login?' — selecting 'No' configures DHCP mode (IPoE), while 'Yes' switches to PPPoE. This wording can confuse customers because it doesn't explicitly mention DHCP or Dynamic IP. For Belong, the answer is always 'No'. The Nighthawk app can handle initial setup but for WAN reconfiguration, the web admin panel (routerlogin.net) is more reliable. The yellow Internet port is well-labeled and hard to confuse with LAN ports.

setup_sources.description: Netgear KB — How to log in (admin/password, routerlogin.net)
Netgear Community — Switching from PPPoE to IPoE/DHCP (login required = No for DHCP)
Netgear — RAX50 User Manual PDF (port layout, factory reset, full UI reference)
Netgear KB — Firmware update via web browser (ADVANCED > Administration > Firmware Update)
Simply Broadband NZ — RAX50 configuration guide (Basic > Internet, VLAN and DHCP steps)

setup_confidence.notes: Admin IP (192.168.1.1 / routerlogin.net) confirmed by Netgear KB and multiple router database sites. Default credentials (admin/password) confirmed by Netgear KB. WAN config approach ('Does your Internet connection require a login?' = No for DHCP) confirmed by Netgear Community forum. Physical port layout (yellow Internet port, 4 LAN, USB 3.0, rear reset button 10sec) confirmed by user manual PDF reference and search results. Firmware path (ADVANCED > Administration > Firmware Update) confirmed by Netgear KB. Score: 100 - 10 (WAN nav path has two alternatives 'Basic > Internet' vs 'ADVANCED > Setup > Internet Setup' — documented both but not verified which applies to RAX50 specifically) - 5 (Netgear manufacturer page JS-rendered, couldn't fetch directly) = 85.
